class Solution {
public:
    vector<int> evenOddBit(int n) {
        int even = 0, odd = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 32; ++i) {
            if ((n >> i) & 1) {
                if (i % 2 == 0) {
                    ++even;
                } else {
                    ++odd;
                }
            }
        }
        return {even, odd};
    }
};

solution: ```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    vector<int> evenOddBit(int n) {
        int even = 0, odd = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 32; ++i) {
            if ((n >> i) & 1) {
                if (i % 2 == 0) {
                    ++even;
                } else {
                    ++odd;
                }
            }
        }
        return {even, odd};
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates through each bit position (0-31) of the integer. For each set bit, it checks if the index is even or odd and increments counters accordingly. This approach leverages fast bit operations and has constant time/space complexity. The main strength is its simplicity and efficiency, while the only minor weakness is processing up to 32 bits even for small numbers.
